evals.name: operator-fixes-and-exponent
evals.prompt: I have a data dictionary that keeps failing to upload. The branching logic has some wrong operators I think. Can you fix it? The file is at /sessions/zealous-intelligent-ritchie/redcap-dd-fixer-workspace/test_dd_operators.csv
evals.expected_output: Should run fix_dd.py, identify == operators in branching logic, convert them to =, convert && to AND, convert != to <>, flag the broken exponentiation in the BMI formula ([weight_kg])/([height_m])^(2) — the base is already wrapped but the weight_kg reference lacks parentheses — and produce a corrected CSV with a change log. Should also flag missing choices on dropdown fields if any, and note the Identifier?=Y normalization.

assertions.name: Ran fix_dd.py script
assertions.description: The response shows evidence of running the fix_dd.py script (not just manually describing issues)

assertions.name: Fixed == to =
assertions.description: The output CSV or change log shows that == was replaced with = in branching logic

assertions.name: Fixed && to AND
assertions.description: The output CSV or change log shows && was replaced with AND

assertions.name: Fixed != to <>
assertions.description: The output CSV or change log shows != was replaced with <>

assertions.name: Produced a corrected CSV file
assertions.description: A corrected CSV file was written and its path was shared with the user

assertions.name: Normalized Identifier Y flag
assertions.description: Reported the normalization of Identifier?=Y to y (or the output CSV reflects this)

evals.name: structural-errors-and-calctext
evals.prompt: Please fix this data dictionary — I got errors when trying to upload it. File: /sessions/zealous-intelligent-ritchie/redcap-dd-fixer-workspace/test_dd_structural.csv
evals.expected_output: Should parse the DD, run the fixer, identify: (1) the missing choices on 'diagnosis' dropdown and 'adverse_event' radio — flagged not auto-fixed; (2) the double quotes in the @CALCTEXT annotation on pat_name — auto-fixed to single quotes; (3) the validation min/max on ae_description (which is a text field so these are actually valid — but min=100 means minimum 100 characters which is unusual, should be noted); (4) the uppercase 'Dropdown' field type — auto-fixed to lowercase; (5) the duplicate record_id variable — flagged as error; (6) the broken @CALCTEXT with escaped double quotes around 'Unnamed'. Should produce a corrected CSV.

assertions.name: Flagged missing choices
assertions.description: Correctly flagged that 'diagnosis' (Dropdown) and 'adverse_event' (radio) have no choices defined

assertions.name: Flagged duplicate variable name
assertions.description: Identified that record_id appears twice and flagged it as an error

assertions.name: Fixed double quotes in @CALCTEXT
assertions.description: Converted double quotes to single quotes inside the @CALCTEXT annotation on pat_name

assertions.name: Produced a corrected CSV file
assertions.description: A corrected CSV file was written and its path was shared with the user

assertions.name: Provided clear summary of fixes and remaining issues
assertions.description: The response clearly separates what was auto-fixed from what still requires manual action

evals.name: mixed-whitespace-and-missing-formulas
evals.prompt: My data dictionary has a bunch of issues — whitespace in variable names, wrong operators, missing choices, missing formulas. Can you fix what you can and tell me what's left? File is at /sessions/zealous-intelligent-ritchie/redcap-dd-fixer-workspace/test_dd_mixed.csv
evals.expected_output: Should parse and run fixer on the mixed issues DD. Should fix: (1) leading/trailing whitespace on ' mrn ' variable name; (2) == operators in fu_date and fu_notes branching logic to =, != to <>; (3) YES in Required Field? normalized to y. Should flag: (4) missing choices on refer_source, smoking, and alcohol fields; (5) missing formula on phq9_total calc field; (6) mrn variable name after stripping whitespace may have issues if the result still has a leading space issue. Should also note the phq9 matrix fields have validation min/max set which is valid since they're text fields. Should deliver corrected CSV.

assertions.name: Fixed whitespace in variable name
assertions.description: Stripped leading/trailing whitespace from ' mrn ' variable name

assertions.name: Fixed == and != operators in branching logic
assertions.description: Converted == to = and != to <> in fu_date and fu_notes branching logic

assertions.name: Normalized YES to y in Required Field
assertions.description: Converted YES → y in the Required Field? column for dob

assertions.name: Flagged fields with missing choices
assertions.description: Flagged refer_source, smoking, and alcohol as missing choices

assertions.name: Flagged calc field with no formula
assertions.description: Flagged phq9_total as a calc field with no formula

assertions.name: Produced a corrected CSV file
assertions.description: A corrected CSV file was written and its path was shared with the user
